htaOutcomes.recommendedPopulation: Canada’s Drug Agency (CDA-AMC) recommends that Carvykti should be reimbursed by public drug plans for the treatment of adult patients with multiple myeloma (MM), who have received 1 to 3 prior lines of therapy, including a proteasome inhibitor and an immunomodulatory drug, andwhose disease is refractory to lenalidomide if certain conditions are met.Carvykti should only be reimbursed for who have a good performance status.Carvykti should only be reimbursed if it is prescribed and administered by clinicians with expertise in the treatment of MM at specialized centres with adequate infrastructure, resources, and expertise to facilitate treatment with chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy.
htaOutcomes.reasonForRecommendation: • Evidence from a clinical trial demonstrated that Carvykti prolonged the time until disease progression or death and was associated with an improved response to treatment.• Based on our assessment of the health economic evidence, Carvykti does not represent good value to the health care system at the public list price. A price reduction is therefore required.• Carvykti met some patient needs because it is a one-time treatment that offers an alternative option, delays disease progression, and may help reduce MM symptoms without worsening the patient’s overall quality of life.• The implementation of Carvykti, including in earlier lines of therapy, may raise several ethical and equity considerations related to access and resource allocation because of the resource-intensive nature of CAR T-cell therapy and the currently limited number of CAR T-cell centres in Canada.• Based on public list prices, Carvykti is estimated to cost public drug plans approximately $477 million over the next 3 years.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, one prior therapy, lenalidomide IR)
htaOutcomes.comments: - Demonstrated significant improvement in progression-free survival (PFS) compared to standard regimens (HR = 0.26).- Achieved higher rates of complete response and minimal residual disease negativity.- Safety profile includes serious adverse events such as cytokine release syndrome, neurotoxicity, cytopenias, and infections.- Benefit most pronounced in patients with 1-3 prior lines of therapy, including those refractory to lenalidomide.- Quality of life improvements observed, but open-label design limits interpretation.
htaOutcomes.reimbursedPopulation: Patients must meet the following criteria:-Have a documented diagnosis of multiple myeloma according to the IMWG diagnostic criteria;-Age ≥ 18 years;-Having received at least 1 prior line of treatment for multiple myeloma;-Having received an immunomodulatory agent and a proteasome inhibitor as part of prior therapy;-Having presented disease progression to the last treatment and being refractory to lenalidomide;-Have good functional status (ECOG Performance Status grade 0 – 1);-Have adequate kidney, liver, bone marrow, heart and lung function;-No previous history of other treatments against BCMA;-Not having received previous treatment with CAR-T therapy-No known active or previous history of central nervous system involvement or clinical signs of meningeal involvement related to multiple myeloma;-No history of Parkinson's disease or other neurodegenerative disorder;-Not having undergone allogeneic hematopoietic progenitor transplantation within 6 months prior to apheresis or ongoing treatment with immunosuppressants, or autologous hematopoietic progenitor transplantation within 12 weeks prior to apheresis;-Patients with a previous history of HIV, hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection must have an undetectable viral load.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, one prior therapy, lenalidomide IR)
htaOutcomes.reimbursedPopulation: The pharmaceutical specialty containing ciltacabtagene autoleucel is eligible for reimbursement when it is infused in a beneficiary aged 18 years or older with multiple myeloma who meets all required characteristics simultaneously. The patient must have received at least one previous treatment, including a proteasome inhibitor and an immunomodulatory agent, and the disease must be refractory to at least lenalidomide. In addition, the disease must have shown progression during the last treatment, the patient must have an ECOG performance status of 0–1, and must not have been previously treated with any T‑cell redirectional therapy, including a CAR‑T cell therapy or a bispecific antibody
htaOutcomes.reimbursementConditions: Reimbursement is granted only when the treatment has been approved in advance by a Multidisciplinary Oncological Consultation (MOC). It is reimbursed as a single, one‑time treatment according to the recommended dose, and only if the product has actually been infused in the beneficiary. The reimbursement excludes the possibility of reimbursing any other BCMA‑directed CAR‑T cell therapy for the same indication. The treatment must be prescribed by a specialist in internal medicine holding the specific professional title in clinical hematology, trained and qualified by the marketing authorization holder. The product must be administered exclusively in a treatment center with sufficient expertise in cell therapy and accredited by the marketing authorization holder. Reimbursement is provided only if the hospital pharmacist has proof of the electronic approval prior to invoicing, and the specialist must commit to cooperating in future registry data collection when available

htaOutcomes.relevantComparison: Treatment combinations that include the drugs Kyprolis, pomalidomide, or Darzalex
htaOutcomes.tlvComments: • The basis for the current health economic assessment is the phase III CARTITUDE-4 study. The study shows that treatment with Carvykti has an efficacy advantage compared to the doctor's choice of standard treatment.• Darzalex and Kyprolis have agreed prices that are subject to confidentiality. As a result, TLV has performed analyses at different price levels for these drugs. Depending on the agreed price of the drugs, the cost per quality-adjusted life year (QALY) gained in TLV's base scenario is between approximately SEK 700,000 and SEK 1 million.• The uncertainty in the data lies primarily in the fact that the effect of treatment with Carvykti in the long term is uncertain. It is also uncertain what subsequent treatment will look like in Sweden.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, one prior therapy, lenalidomide IR)
htaOutcomes.comments: - According to Fimea's clinical expert, the CARTITUDE-4 results are clinically significant compared to reference treatments.- The patients participating in the study were relatively young and in good health (average age 60 years) compared to the usual multiple myeloma patients.- For this reason, a positive study result may not be fully generalizable to the entire multiple myeloma patient population.- No new safety findings emerged from the CARTITUDE-4 study compared to previously known ones.
htaOutcomes.icerValues: In the MAH's baseline analysis, the incremental cost-effectiveness ratio (ICER) of cilta-cel compared to the comparator treatment (DPd/PVd) from the CARTITUDE-4 study is approximately €73,000/QALY. Compared to DKd, cilta-cel is dominant (–€58,000/QALY), meaning that cilta-cel produces more quality-adjusted life years at lower costs. In the scenario analyses, the ICER relative to the DPd/PVd comparator treatment was €49,100–€83,700/QALY and relative to DKd, –€81,200…–€14,800/QALY with cilta-cel dominating.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, one prior therapy, lenalidomide IR)
htaOutcomes.assessmentOutcomeComments: SMR: Important➔ Multiple myeloma is a serious, life-threatening blood disorder.➔ This is a curative drug.➔ The efficacy/adverse effects ratio is high.➔ This is a second-line treatment for multiple myeloma, given available therapies.➔ Public health benefitGiven:‒ the severity of the disease,‒ the partially met medical need,‒ the partial response, given:• a demonstrated additional impact on morbidity and mortality and quality of life. However, uncertainties remain regarding the long-term efficacy and safety of this drugdue to limited data collection; • Given the lack of demonstrated impact on the organization of care,CARVYKTI (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) is not likely to have an additional impact on public health.Taking all of these factors into account, the Commission considers that the medical benefitprovided by CARVYKTI (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) is substantial within the MA indication. The Commission issues a favorable opinion for the inclusion of CARVYKTI (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) on the list of proprietary medicinal products approved for hospital use in the MA indication and at the MA dosages.ASMR IIIGiven:‒ the demonstration in a randomized, open-label, phase 3 study of the superiority ofCARVYKTI (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) compared to DPd or PVd protocols (at the investigator's choice) in terms of:• progression-free survival (primary endpoint) assessed by a computer algorithmvalidated under the responsibility of a laboratory that remained blinded to the assigned treatment, and definedby the IMWG criteria: HR = 0.26 (95% CI: [0.18; 0.38]), p-value < 0.0001 without taking into accountprogression events during the first 8 weeks, otherwise HR = 0.40 (95% CI: [0.29; 0.55]);• Complete response or stringent complete response rate assessed by a computerized algorithm (ranked secondary endpoint): OR = 10.3; 95% CI: [6.5; 16.4]; p < 0.0001;• Overall response rate (ranked secondary endpoint): OR = 3.0; 95% CI: [1.8; 5.0]; p < 0.0001;• Overall minimal residual disease (MRD) negative rate (ranked secondary endpoint): OR = 8.7; 95% CI: [5.4; 13.9]; p < 0.0001;• overall survival (prioritized secondary endpoint) at the second interim analysis performed on 01/05/2024 with HR = 0.55; 95% CI: [0.39; 0.79]; p = 0.0009 <0.0108;• time to symptom worsening according to the MySIm-Q score with 79 deteriorations observed (30 in the cilta-cel group and 49 in the control group) and HR = 0.38; 95% CI:[0.24; 0.61]; p < 0.0001. The median time to score deterioration was 23.7 months in the cilta-cel group and 18.86 months in the control group;and despite:‒ a crossover in the progression-free survival curves, with a higher number of progressions in the cilta-cel group during the first 8 weeks before cell injection,without a clear explanation, a crossover that is also reflected in the overall survival curves,thus suggesting an increase in early mortality while awaiting reinjection, before a potentialdelayed benefit;‒ a safety profile marked in particular by neurotoxicity and a long-term risk ofsecondary hematological cancer, requiring more data to resolve uncertaintiesregarding the general longer-term safety profile of this CAR-T; ‒ the impossibility of positioning it in relation to ABECMA (idecabtagene vicleucel) in patients with their second or subsequent relapse of multiple myeloma, in the absence of robust data,the Commission considers that CARVYKTI (idecabtagene autoleucel) provides a moderate improvement in actual benefit (IAB III) compared to the DPd [daratumumab – pomalidomide – dexamethasone] and PVd [pomalidomide – bortezomib – dexamethasone] protocols in the treatment of adult patients with relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma, who have received at least one prior treatment, including an immunomodulatory agent and a proteasome inhibitor, who are refractory to lenalidomide and whose disease has progressed during the last treatment.

htaOutcomes.comments: - Clinical studies have demonstrated the superiority of this therapy over currently reimbursed technologies.- There are strong clinical recommendations supporting its use.- The drug is associated with a very high price, and there are no satisfactory risk-sharing mechanisms proposed.- There is significant uncertainty regarding the estimated size of the patient population.- Overall, while the therapy shows clear clinical benefits and is recommended by experts, the financial implications and lack of robust risk-sharing strategies raise concerns. Additionally, the uncertainty about how many patients may require this treatment complicates budget planning and decision-making for reimbursement authorities.

htaOutcomes.icerValues: NCPE adjusted base case ICERs: kilta-cel versus car+dex: €77,024 per QALY; cilta-cel versus dar+bor+dex: €90,972 per QALY; cilta-cel versus pom+bor+dex: €94,919 per QALY; cilta-cel versus pom+dex: €106,658 per QALY.
htaOutcomes.priceToBeReducedToBecomeCostEffetive: Percentage reductions between 43% and 86% (depending on the comparator) would be required to meet the €45,000 per QALY cost-effectiveness threshold. Percentage reductions of between 70% and 95% would be required to meet the €20,000 per QALY threshold.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, TCE, atleast three prior lines)
htaOutcomes.reasonForRecommendation: MSAC did not accept that cilta-cel is comparatively safe, effective and cost-effective over the modelled time horizon. MSAC also considered the low level of clinical evidence in support of cilta-cel to be unacceptable in the context of late-line treatment where other treatment options are available, and the prevalence of RRMM being clearly beyond that of a rare disease, with a large and uncertain financial impact.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, TCE, atleast three prior lines)
htaOutcomes.recommendedPopulation: Carvykti should only be reimbursed for patients who have not yet been treated with chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy if it is prescribed and administered in a hospital setting with adequate resources by specialists with expertise in MM.
htaOutcomes.reasonForRecommendation: • Evidence from a clinical trial suggested that treatment with Carvykti resulted in durable responses to treatment within 1 month of administration and may improve overall survival time and the time until disease progression or death. Furthermore, evidence from 5 observational studies consistently suggested that Carvykti may improve response rate, overall survival time, time until disease progression or death, and time until another treatment is required compared with other treatments used in the real world. • Carvykti may meet some patient needs because it is an additional life-extending treatment option and has manageable side effects. • The implementation of Carvykti may raise several ethical and equity considerations related to access because of the resource-intensive Summary nature of CAR T-cell therapy and the currently limited number of CAR T-cell centres. • Based on CADTH’ s assessment of the health economic evidence, Carvykti does not represent good value to the health care system at the public list price. A price reduction is therefore required. • Based on public list prices, Carvykti is estimated to cost the public drug plans approximately $405 million over the next 3 years. However, the actual budget impact is uncertain and may be higher.• Carvykti should not be reimbursed to treat patients whose MM is affecting their brain or spinal cord or patients showing signs that the tissue layers protecting the brain and spinal cord are affected by MM. It also should not be reimbursed in patients who have previously received a treatment that targets B-cell maturation antigen.• Carvykti should not be initiated in patients with active CNS involvement or exhibiting signs of meningeal involvement of MM.• Carvykti should not be reimbursed in patients who have received prior treatment with any therapy that is targeted to BCMA or any CAR T-cell therapy.• Carvykti should only be reimbursed if the cost of Carvykti is reduced.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, TCE, atleast three prior lines)
htaOutcomes.reasonForRecommendation: MSAC considered that the estimated costs associated with cilta-cel therapy were highly uncertain and underestimated. MSAC deferred its advice to see whether a lower ICER could be achieved through adjusting inputs in the economic model (particularly hospital costs and health benefits gained - to be verified by jurisdictions) and for a significant price reduction to be offered by the company. MSAC advised that an ICER in the range of those for other treatments in later line RRMM recommended by PBAC would be more likely to be acceptable. Furthermore, MSAC requested the Department negotiate a pay-for-performance arrangement to incentivise payment on the performance of the deeper level of clinical response achieved at 12-months after cilta-cel infusion.

htaOutcomes.assessmentOutcomeComments: SMR: Important➔ Multiple myeloma is a serious and life-threatening blood disease.➔ The proprietary medicinal product Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) is a medicinal product with curative intent.➔ The efficacy/adverse effects ratio is high.➔ There are therapeutic alternatives (see section 05 of this opinion).➔ Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) is a treatment of 4 th or more line of myeloma relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma, in patients who have received at least three prior treatments, including an immunomodulatory agent, a proteasome inhibitor and an anti-CD38 antibody, and whose disease has progressed during the last treatment.➔ Public health interestConsidering:‒ the severity of the disease and its incidence,‒ partially covered medical need,‒ the lack of response to the identified need,‒ the absence of any evidence to substantiate an absence of degradation of the course of care/and or life, considering in particular the systematic hospitalization during the first 14 days following the infusion and the need for patients to remain close to the qualified health establishment for at least 4 weeks after the infusion,‒ the lack of data enabling its impact on the organization of care to be assessed, considering in particular the obligation to accredit health establishments according to specific criteria and enhanced coordination between the different services of the centers authorized to administer the CAR-T cells (laboratories, pharmacy for indoor use, clinical services, etc.) Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) is not likely to have a public health benefit.Taking into account all of these elements, the Commission considers that the medical service delivered by Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) is important in the Marketing Authorization indication.The Committee gives a favorable opinion on the inclusion on the list of specialties approved for hospital use in the indication and at the dosages in the Marketing Authorisation.ASMR VConsidering :‒ efficacy data from a non-comparative phase 1/2 study, with a rate of overall response of 83% [95% CI: 75-90] in the ITT population, in an engaging situation the life-threatening,‒ uncertainties concerning the clinical relevance of the primary endpoint used (rate overall response) and its transferability to the duration of overall survival or the improvement of life quality,‒ uncertainties about the quantity of effect specific to this treatment, considering the methodological weaknesses of indirect comparisons,‒ the safety profile marked by significant toxicity in the short and medium term,‒ uncertainties about clinical efficacy and longer-term tolerance,‒ and despite the interest of having a drug that has been evaluated after treatment failure prior art including an immunomodulatory agent, a proteasome inhibitor and an antibody anti-CD38.The Transparency Committee considers that in the current state of the data, and pending in particular the results of the randomized phase III CARTITUDE-4 study, Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel), like ABECMA (idecabtagene vicleucel), does not bring any improvement actual benefit (ASMR V) in the management of adult patients with relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma, having received at least three previous treatments, including immunomodulatory agent, proteasome inhibitor and anti-CD38 antibody and whose disease progressed during the last treatment.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, TCE, atleast three prior therapies, previous Tx IR)
htaOutcomes.tlvComments: • The basis for the current health economic assessment is the CARTITUDE-1 study. Since CARTITUDE-1 lacked a control group, the results from this study have been indirectly compared in a matched comparison with data from the Loco M Motion study of patients treated with standard treatment.• The matched indirect comparisons showed a statistically significantly better effect of Carvykti regarding how patients respond to treatment (overall response rate, ORR), how long the patient lives without the disease getting worse (progression-free survival, PFS) and how long the patient lives (overall survival, OS).• In TLV's base scenario, the cost per quality-adjusted life year (QALY) gained is approximately SEK 1.2 million for Carvykti compared to standard treatment. The uncertainty in the results is assessed as very high and lies primarily in the estimate of overall survival and the indirect comparison used in the analysis.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, TCE, atleast three prior therapies, previous Tx IR)
htaOutcomes.comments: • The results in the primary variable, ORR of 97.9% in the treated population and 84.1% in the leukapheresis population, are relevant, and its effect has also been shown in key secondary variables, in particular, the CR rate 82.5% in the treated population and 69.0% in the ITT population, showing consistent results over time. • Regarding other secondary variables, the results in the EMR indicate that the majority of complete responders achieved deep responses to therapy (43.3%), although this value may be underestimated. With a median follow-up of 27.7 months, the median DOR was not reached, which is indicative of long responses, but should be conveniently confirmed with long-term studies.• Until more long-term efficacy and safety data are available, as well as data compared with other therapeutic alternatives, cilta-cel is presented as a treatment option among the available therapeutic alternatives.• Cilta-cel should be administered in accredited facilities and therapy should be performed under the supervision of a professional with experience in the treatment of hematologic malignancies and specific training in the use of this product. Prior to infusion of cilta-cel, at least one dose of tocilizumab for use in case of CRS (or appropriate measures in their absence), and an emergency kit should be available.

htaOutcomes.assessedLabelPopulation: MM (RR, TCE, atleast three prior therapies, previous Tx IR)
htaOutcomes.comments: - The payer believes introducing the treatment is desirable, but the supplier's price is considered excessively high compared to the documented benefits.- Hospital purchasing is requested to reopen negotiations with the supplier to seek a more reasonable price.- If significant new information arises—such as data on patient safety, cost-effectiveness, price changes, availability of biosimilars or generics, or updated survival data—that could alter the current assessment, the decision may be revisited.- The overall stance is that while the treatment has potential value, its current cost does not justify its benefits, and further negotiation or new evidence is needed to reconsider the outcome.

htaOutcomes.reimbursedPopulation: Not reimbursed, unless price negotiation results in at least a 35% price reduction so that the ICER falls below the maximum reference value of 80,000 euros per QALY. A pay for performance agreement is advised to be explored.
htaOutcomes.zinlComments: - The committee recommends a price arrangement with a duration of 5 years, so that during any subsequent price negotiation, the long-term effects of cilta-cel and the initial results of the research into on-site production of CAR-T cells will be known.- A pay for performance agreement is recommended.

htaOutcomes.comments: - Clinical studies have demonstrated the superiority of this therapy over currently reimbursed technologies.- There are strong clinical recommendations supporting its use.- The drug is associated with a very high price, and there are no satisfactory risk-sharing mechanisms proposed.- There is significant uncertainty regarding the estimated size of the patient population.- Overall, while the therapy shows clear clinical benefits and is recommended by experts, the financial implications and lack of robust risk-sharing strategies raise concerns. Additionally, the uncertainty about how many patients may require this treatment complicates budget planning and decision-making for reimbursement authorities.
